concepts.title: Paths and homotopy
concepts.blurb: Continuous paths I→X, homotopy rel endpoints, and the concatenation that makes loop classes a group.

concepts.title: Simply connected domains
concepts.blurb: Path-connected space with trivial fundamental group — the domain hypothesis behind existence theorems in complex analysis.

concepts.title: Fundamental group
concepts.blurb: π₁(X,x₀): loop classes under concatenation. π₁(S¹)=ℤ, basepoint-change, functoriality.

concepts.title: Universal cover of S¹
concepts.blurb: The exponential map $p: \mathbb{R} \to S^1$, $t \mapsto e^{2\pi i t}$, is a covering with discrete fibre $\mathbb{Z}$. Lifting loops to paths in $\mathbb{R}$ converts the topology of $S^1$ into integer arithmetic — every loop has a winding number, and that's the iso $\pi_1(S^1) \cong \mathbb{Z}$.

concepts.title: Covering spaces
concepts.blurb: Locally trivial surjections p:E→B with discrete fibers; the Galois correspondence between covers of B and subgroups of π₁(B).

concepts.title: Singular homology
concepts.blurb: Chains, boundaries, Hₙ(X) as a homotopy invariant; Mayer–Vietoris and the Euler characteristic.
